prompt: Write a JavaScript function compareSemver(a, b) comparing two semantic version strings (no build metadata), returning -1 if a<b, 0 if equal, 1 if a>b. Full SemVer precedence: compare major.minor.patch numerically; a version WITH a prerelease is lower than the same version without one; prerelease identifiers (dot-separated) compare left to right — numeric identifiers compare as numbers, alphanumeric ones lexically, numeric identifiers are always lower than alphanumeric ones, and if all shared identifiers are equal the longer prerelease list wins. Return only the code, no explanation.

Write a JavaScript function parseDuration(s) that parses an ISO-8601 duration limited to days/hours/minutes/seconds (forms like P2D, PT1H30M, P1DT2H3M4.5S) and returns the total number of seconds. Fractional values are allowed on seconds. Throw an Error for anything else: years/months/weeks designators, a bare 'P', a 'T' with nothing after it, missing 'P', or any malformed string. Note the ambiguity trap: 'M' before the 'T' section would mean months (unsupported — throw), 'M' after 'T' means minutes. Return only the code, no explanation.

Write a JavaScript function mergeIntervals(intervals) taking an array of [start, end] integer pairs (each with start <= end, possibly unsorted) and returning a new array of merged, sorted, non-overlapping intervals. Intervals that merely touch (e.g. [1,2] and [2,3]) merge into one. The input array and its inner pairs must NOT be mutated. An empty input returns an empty array. Return only the code, no explanation.

Write a JavaScript function parseCsvLine(line) that splits ONE CSV line into an array of field strings per RFC 4180 quoting rules: fields are comma-separated; a field wrapped in double quotes may contain commas; a doubled quote inside a quoted field is an escaped literal quote; unquoted whitespace is preserved as-is. An empty line yields one empty field. A trailing comma yields a trailing empty field. Return only the code, no explanation.
